title: The Destruction Problem
body: When you try to make subsidiary awareness focal — when you attend to how you're doing the thing instead of the thing — you switch from running the prior to attempting to read it out. The readout disrupts the inference. The prior worked because it operated in the background. Introspection is not access; it's interference.

title: What Expertise Looks Like From Inside
body: A novice has weak priors and relies on evidence. They follow rules, attend to feedback, make errors consciously. An expert has strong priors that run ahead of sensation. Action becomes top-down. Skill acquisition is literally the process of converting propositional knowledge into prior structure. You cannot teach it by telling. You can only provide the right environment long enough for the distribution to reshape.

title: The Prior Is the Knowledge
body: Polanyi said we can know more than we can tell. Predictive coding says why: tacit knowledge is stored as a prior — a probability distribution over high-dimensional space. Distributions cannot be compressed into propositions without catastrophic loss. You cannot read out a prior; you can only watch what it does.

title: Routes From Noise
body: Start with a particle walking a grid at random. Make it slightly prefer cells it has visited before. Run it long enough. Channels emerge. The routes weren't designed — they condensed out of preference and repetition, the way salmon runs condense out of preference and gradient. The path is a record of what was already familiar.

title: Every Codec Is a Theory
body: MP3 throws away what it assumes you can't hear. Bartlett's subjects threw away the ghost because their schema had no slot for it. Every medium of cultural transmission encodes a theory of what the receiver already knows — and when the theory is wrong, the failure is invisible to the sender and catastrophic to the content.

title: The Familiarity Gradient
body: At every fork in the river, the salmon isn't deciding — it's following the branch that processes most fluently. The gradient of familiarity, encoded once at birth, enacted across an ocean. This is what Zajonc was measuring in the laboratory with Chinese characters. The same algorithm, different scale.

title: The Synapse Doesn't Fetch
body: A synaptic weight is not looked up from memory and loaded into a processor. The weight IS the physical structure of the synapse. Storage and processing are identical. This is why the brain runs on 20 watts. There is no bus.

title: 90% Is Movement
body: Approximately 90% of the energy consumed during AI inference is spent moving data between memory and compute. John Backus named this the von Neumann bottleneck in 1977. Carver Mead proposed an architecture without it in 1990. The brain solved it 500 million years earlier.

title: The Scholars Left First
body: In 145 BC, Ptolemy VIII expelled Alexandria's scholars. The scrolls remained for four more centuries. We've been blaming the wrong fire.

title: The Proof of Qualia
body: A synesthesia test and a zero-knowledge proof are structurally identical: you demonstrate possession of an inner state through behavioral consistency, without ever disclosing what having it feels like. All testimony about experience is this. We do not share qualia. We accumulate rounds until doubt becomes irrational.
